campaignWindow.purpose: Prepare factual GCA market-preheat content around the Go China narrative, fixed-supply Base identity, planned member access, and risk-first product buildout without investment promises.

text: GCA security boundary update is ready for review.
GCA contract facts are public: fixed supply, verified source, no blacklist, no pause, no transfer tax, and no hidden mint path.
Current boundary: no completed third-party audit yet. BaseScan token profile publication is still pending official review, while the owner package was refreshed on 2026-06-06.
Official links first. No price or return claim.
nextCopyReadyDraft.claimBoundary: Public security-boundary update only; review before posting and do not add price, return, listing, audit-completion, wallet-vendor, or BaseScan approval claims.

contentQueue.angle: Position the radar as the repeating research format for narrative education and risk-first market observation.
Explain the planned 1,000,000 GCA 30-day holding rule, credits ledger, and GCA Member review path without saying public claiming is live.
Remind users to verify Base Mainnet, the contract address, official links, and the GCA/USDT route.
Connect the GCA concept to non-custodial quant risk tools such as liquidation replay, backtesting, and position sizing.
Point users to the current official GCA/USDT route and disclose starter-depth liquidity and possible slippage.
Reinforce fixed supply, no minting, and the owner reserve disclosure without overstating circulating supply.
State the contract-control facts and make clear no third-party audit has been completed yet.
Describe GCA AI Quant Access as the planned product direction and link to the public release gates.
Tell users where to find official X, Telegram, docs, and safe moderator replies.
Summarize the month of public build work and link back to the announcement ledger.

allowedAngles: Go China Access narrative and China-facing Web3 research direction.
Fixed-supply Base Mainnet token facts and official GCA/USDT route.
Member access, credits, and review workflows as planned utility buildout.
Risk-first education, public documentation, and transparent operations.
doNotPublish: return promises, price targets, market predictions, or unsupported investment-style claims
third-party audit claim until an independent report is public
BaseScan or security-vendor approval claim unless the platform confirms it
artificial activity language, LP lock claim, or reserve lock claim before evidence exists
